title: Magnetic Electrochemical (Mag-EC) Enzyme Linked Immunosorbent Assay
description:
Point-of-care medical diagnostic technologies to store, process, and analyze a variety of biological and non-biological samples is a critical need for future human space exploration to enable in-flight diagnosis, monitoring, and treatment of spaceflight medical conditions in real-time and inform early interventions for exploration missions with limited or complete inability for timely sample return and sample storage.

The Magnetic Electrochemical (Mag-EC) Enzyme Linked Immunosorbent As
technologyArea: Human Health, Life Support, and Habitation Systems

title: Crew Health and Performance Integrated Data System Platform
description: Future exploration missions to Mars will have increased need for crew autonomy. Crew Health & Performance (CHP) related data on the ISS is currently downlinked manually and is in disparate locations, which limits crew autonomy for future missions. The CHP-IDSP project is developing a backend data system platform that grants the ability to seamlessly collect, store, process, and display CHP-related data for exploration missions. This platform allows for integration of data and advanced analyt
technologyArea: Human Health, Life Support, and Habitation Systems

title: Multi-Functional Integrated Medical Device
description: The current ISS medical system has a large mass and volume footprint. Provision of care for several significant medical conditions is out of scope for the current system given the ability to quickly evacuate from ISS back to ground. As missions increase in both duration and distance, so too does medical risk. To combat this increased risk, the medical system needs to be more capable while also conforming to mass and volume constraints. The Multi-functional Integrated Medical (MIM) device pro

title: Mini Optical Coherence Tomography
description:
Spaceflight Associated Neuro-ocular Syndrome (SANS) Diagnostic Devices: Mini Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT)

Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT) is a non-invasive diagnostic technique that renders an in vivo cross-sectional view of the retina. OCT is used terrestrially in optometry and ophthalmology clinics as a diagnostic tool.

Spaceflight Associated Neuro-ocular Syndrome (SANS) is an important spaceflight risk that NASA is studying to better understand if miti

title: Spacesuit Fit & Injury Technologies
description: Exploration (Lunar and Mars) surface Extravehicular Activities (EVA)s will have physically and cognitively demanding tasks for crew with high injury risk. In addition, crews will perform a larger quantity of EVAs with far less rest between EVAs than ever before. The Spacesuit Fit & Injury Technologies (SFIT) work develops suit-independent, generalizable tools to predict, monitor, and mitigate EVA crewmember injury. The SFIT project will prioritize technology development based on the Injur
technologyArea: Human Health, Life Support, and Habitation Systems

title: Advanced Trace Contaminant Control
description: Trace Contaminant Control (TCC) is typically activated carbon and some level of catalyst to adsorb and react contaminants in the air phase. The current state-of-the-art carbon dioxide (CO2) removal sorbents utilize amines that rely on a hard vacuum to desorb during an Extravehicular Activity (EVA). Some sorbents also act to remove some trace contaminants. This may be severely limited on Mars due to the atmosphere. Therefore, Mars will require new methods, sorbents, and testing of the

title: Mars Portable Life Support System Testbed
description:
To get from Moon to Mars, development of a Mars-centered spacesuit to support extravehicular activity (EVA) must begin now. In an effort towards this goal, setup of a Mars Portable Life Support System (PLSS) team to evaluate schematics for a Mars Extravehicular Mobility Unit (MxEMU) is underway. As part of a previous study, a wide range of potential architectures were evaluated and compared to determine leading options for a Martian PLSS.


Going forward, these schematics will infor

title: Crew Health And Performance Exploration Analog
description: Missions for Moon to Mars are very different from missions to the ISS due to length of isolation and confinement, distance from Earth, and the resource restrictions that will be applied to the crew. Artemis Program vehicles have limited mass allocations for food and other consumables. Given the increased distance to Mars and timeline for return, programs need to understand the impacts of restrictions within realistic constraints on health and performance to inform risk/resource trades prior t

title: Crew Health And Performance Exploration Analog (CHAPEA) Mission 2
description: Missions for Moon to Mars are very different from missions to the ISS due to length of isolation and confinement, distance from Earth, and the resource restrictions that will be applied to the crew. Artemis Program vehicles have limited mass allocations for food and other consumables. Given the increased distance to Mars and timeline for return, programs need to understand the impacts of restrictions within realistic constraints on health and performance to inform risk/resource trades prior t
technologyArea: Human Health, Life Support, and Habitation Systems

title: Joint Observations Integrated Network : Modular software components to support the DOME's multi-mission data processing needs
description: This project supports Goddard ESD's DOME approach, advances GSFC 2040 Vector 3's vision for pioneering data integration and supports a more integrated and efficient approach to Earth science and mission operations. We aim to overcome challenges posed by data heterogeneity and disaggregation to enable more comprehensive scientific understanding and support advanced mission concepts. To do this, we propose to develop the Joint Observations Integrated Network (JOIN), a software system for combining
technologyArea: Tools and Methodologies for Software Design and Development

title: Real-Time Signal Integrity Monitoring
description: The process for evaluating rocket engine test data is time-intensive and reactive. Operators must process the resulting data before sending it for manual review by analysts which can take several hours to days. Anomalies or performance deviations may go unnoticed until much later in the analysis cycle which impacts test efficiency. Develop an AI-driven configuration assistant that learns Expected Signal Behavior and detects Deviations in Real-Time and  Integrates AI signal modeling and deviation

title: Volatile Release from Mars/Lunar Regolith by Mechanical Agitation
description: Proposal will investigate the extent to which mechanical agitation causes release of simulated solar wind particles implanted in lunar regolith simulant by crushing or grinding the material, or by normal surface operations such as excavation, geotechnical testing, or regolith conveying.

title: Development of a COMPact Space weather Sensor payload
description: We propose developing COMPASS, a compact space weather sensor suite integrating novel 3D-printed miniaturized science grade magnetometer technology for comprehensive Space Situational Awareness. The system provides critical measurements of particle fluxes, magnetic fields, and radiation while dramatically reducing SWaP enabling mass production through additive manufacturing techniques of conductive coils and ceramic structure. COMPASS targets deployment across commercial and government satellite

title: (GCA-1 with L3Harris): Lunar and Martian Regolith Testing of Antenna Systems and Associated Mechanisms
description: This project will focus on the development and implementation of test protocols and dust mitigation strategies for lunar and Martian regolith effects on tracking and deployable high gain antenna systems and associated mechanisms with an emphasis on extending system life. This project will be completed via a partnership between NASA Goddard and L3Harris to develop critical communications systems that align to the Goddard/SMD Tier 1 capability of supporting Space Networks and evolution. The propos

title: Improved Efficiency Quantum-Enhanced Very Long Baseline Interferometry Telescopes
description: Capability Need/Knowledge Gap:   Improved imaging of dim astrophysics phenomena, particularly for habitable Earth-like exoplanets, and higher precision, particularly for Mars mapping and deep-space navigation. Related to Civil Space Shortfalls 1598 and 1604, as well as 1626     Objectives: Improve imaging, including reducing the number of samples required to achieve given resolution, through advancing  Quantum-Enhanced Very Long Baseline Interferometry (QE-VLBI)

title: High Lift Rotor Blades for Mars Helicopters
description: Capability Need/Knowledge Gap:   To enable Mars helicopters that can carry more science payload, rotors that have higher blade area (high solidity) are necessary. There is little research for Earth-based helicopters with high solidity.        Objectives:  (1) Develop design guidelines and rotor designs for high solidity rotors for Mars helicopters,  (2) Show and publish high solidity blade impact on heli performance and exploration capabilities

title: IMPedance Analysis & Certification Technnology
description: Proposed Goals/Objectives:  (1) Integrate EIT with TPS certification,  (2) Extend 2D EIT to system scale (>24”).      Stretch: develop impedance correlation models for other TPS material properties (thermal conductivity, phase, strain) via impedance spectra to enhance EIT cert. & diagnostic capabilities.     Vision: Low cost and rapid in-situ certification of heatshields at any scale including Human M2M missions.

title: AIR (Ames Infrared) Imager Maturation
description: Proposed Goals/Objectives:   Goal: Continue maturation of the versatile cost-effective Ames IR (AIR) imager; a tunable and robust hyperspectral imager with a spectral range from the visible to near infrared wavelengths to the short wavelength infrared (~0.4-2.1 μm). AIR is an in-house, first of its kind compact imager for space-based applications with no moving parts and low power/mass/cost; benefits exploration of our solar system, aligning with the NASA strategic documents, decadal survey, an

title: Feasibility of Earth-Based Testing for Mars Rotorcraft Dynamics
description: Capability Need/Knowledge Gap:    Existing methods and testing facilities to perform flight testing/V&V are insufficient for next-gen Mars rotorcraft which have >15x mass of Ingenuity. Performing sub-scale vehicle testing in Earth’s atmosphere conditions, while replicating on[1]planet flight dynamics, remains unexplored.      Goal: Mature sub-scale vehicle testing in Earth's atmosphere conditions to enable fast, cost[1]effective flight dynamics V&V of large planetary rotorcraft

title: Efficient Learning for Control of Autonomous Mobility in Navigating Obstacles (EL-CAMINO
description: Capability Need/Knowledge Gap: ESDMD, SMD, and STMD have all highly ranked the gap in high progress rate autonomous surface mobility. Systems must travel at unprecedented speeds using new sensing and computing, requiring more capable algorithms.   Objectives: Train faster and more capable onboard localization algorithms to learn models for combining camera images and lidar scans leveraging recent advances in model-based learning from terrestrial robotics

title: LuxBio: Light-based biological sensing and control for automated deep-space experimentation
description: Proposed Goals/Objectives: The BioSentinel/LEIA BioSensor, an automated microbiological payload with deep-space flight heritage, supports  microbiological experiments essential to long-term space exploration. Our FY25 IRAD, “Fluorescence detection and optogenetic activation for microbial experimentation beyond LEO,” laid the groundwork to expand its measurement capabilities from just optical absorbance to fluorescence, a more versatile biological reporter; and demonstrated the use of light to co

title: CoOptiSense – Cooperative Optimized Sensor Fusion for Next-Gen Rover Navigation
description: Capability Need/Knowledge Gap:  (1) Next-gen rover missions will utilize both cameras & LIDAR to navigate, but traditional sensor fusion approaches collect too much 3D data for real-time processing.  (2) Active cooperation between camera & LIDAR can generate optimized 3D maps, with dense useful data in crucial areas and less wasted measurement overall.        Objectives: Demonstrate cooperative camera-LIDAR fusion for navigation: using complementary sensors to collect 3D data where its needed mo

title: Enabling Automated Production of Near-Net Shape Felted TPS up to 2m Scale
description: Proposed Goals/Objectives:  The proposed effort will automate needling processes for all variants in the Materials Engineered for Re-entry using Innovative Needling Operations (MERINO) family of TPS.   (1) Apply the (currently idle) multi-million-dollar Robotic Arm facility in N210 for optimized fiber placement and felt production,   (2) Radically increase production rates and reduce variability of material properties,   (3) Fabricate a large (~2m) near-net shape MERINO-LD part that fully utiliz

title: FY26 IRAD : Detection and Characterization of Single Mag-EC ELISAConstructs for Ultra-Low LOD Life Detection
description: Proposed Goals/Objectives:  Our goal is to better understand the physics governing Mag-EC ELISA detection.  Our hypothesis is that we will be able to measure individual bead constructs as they impact an electrode surface. Further, we believe that we can leverage this phenomenon by using a large amount of Mag-EC ELISA constructs to ensure capture target molecules throughout the solution and rapidly draw all the constructs to the electrode for detection.   If we can successfully develop analytical

title: TABS: Transfer-line Acoustic Bubble Sensing
description: Capability Need/Knowledge Gap:    There's a critical need to detect harmful bubbles inside opaque cryogenic transfer lines for NTP missions, as current methods can't, risking turbopump failure and engine restarts. A real-time detection capability is missing.   Objectives:    Calibrate acoustic sensors on a water rig using video ground truth; derive a conservative bubble/no[1]bubble alarm threshold (ΔΓ) based on N₂ bubbles; benchmark this alarm's yes/no detection against video data.

title: FY26 ARIA:  Multi-Analyte Prediction of Bone Fracture
description: Proposed Goals/Objectives:   There is a need for longitudinal monitoring of body’s physiological response, such as bone quality, to spaceflight. Traditional bone biomarkers have known limitations, thus the need for investigation of novel biomarkers of skeletal health. MicroRNA (miRNA) are short, noncoding sequences that regulate gene expression, including during osteoblast, osteocyte, and osteoclast differentiation.     The objective of the continuation proposal is to expand the sample size and

title: Enabling a high-contrast imaging testbed for Habitable Worlds Observatory
description: Proposed Goals/Objectives: Enable a high-contrast imaging testbed for NASA’s Habitable Worlds Observatory (HWO) by re-utilizing and upgrading the SOFIA vacuum chamber. HWO will be the first observatory to directly image Earth-like planets around sun-like stars and search for signs of life with a goal of finding 25 habitable planets.

title: Radiation Biomarker Detection in Breath
description: Proposed Goals/Objectives:   Spaceflight health monitoring lacks compact, high-sensitivity tools to detect biomarkers of radiation-induced oxidative stress in real time. We aim to advance our carbon nanotube (CNT) sensor system for detecting radiation-induced volatile organic compounds (VOCs) by optimizing both hardware and software to increase sensitivity and specificity. FY26 work will enhance hardware, software, and sample handling; implement selective sensor filtering; and add calibration to

title: R5 Program Flight Demo of COMLINC
description: Perform a LEO flight demo of the COMLINC unit, demonstrating unit downlink capability and envelope performance across a variety of ground stations

title: Advanced Electric Field/Langmuir Probe System for Measuring DC/AC Electric Fields, Dust and Debris Impacts, Lightning Sferics and Transmitters, and Sheaths and Spacecraft Potential in Very Low Earth Orbit environments
description: This IRAD develops advanced electric field detectors for very low earth orbit (VLEO) missions, in particular the IARPA VLEO mission in which Goddard expects to provide a combined electric field/Langmuir probe/magnetometer suite in addition to other instrumentation.  The detector will measure DC/AC electric fields, spacecraft charging, dust and debris impacts, lightning sferics, VLF and HF transmitters, solitons and lower hybrid waves from the wake of charged sub-cm debris, as well as the effects

title: WATCH: Wearable Astronaut Tribocharge Hazard monitor
description: The WATCH (Wearable Astronaut Tribocharge Hazard monitor) project will develop and demonstrate a compact sensor system to detect and warn astronauts of lunar tribocharging hazards in real time. This technology directly supports Artemis missions by mitigating risk associated with electrostatic discharge, meeting strategic SFA goals for human exploration safety. The proposed IRAD will perform a concept study to scope the requirements and baseline design of WATCH. With overguide funding, we will ai

title: Plasma Emission Spectroscopy for Planetary Geochemistry, Enabled by Robotic Arm-Mounted Laser Ablation Sampling
description: We propose to conduct a concept study on quantification of elemental abundances using laser ablation microwave-induced plasma optical emission spectroscopy (LA-MIP-OES) for in situ geochemistry of planetary surfaces. This analytical technique will incorporate a novel sampling method for rocky surface material by interfacing a sealable laser ablation sampling cell to a robotic arm, providing greater ease of sample selection than previous missions and concepts. This effort will establish calibrati

title: Mars Scoreboards for Space Weather Nowcasts and Forecasts
description: To safeguard Mars crewed and robotic missions, the Community Coordinated Modeling Center (CCMC) will develop three public state-of-the-art space weather forecasting Scoreboard web applications for Mars, focusing on Solar Energetic Particles (SEPs), Coronal Mass Ejections (CMEs), and Solar Indices. The Mars Scoreboards work will provide critical space weather forecasts and characterize the environment to assess and mitigate astronaut radiation risks, communication disruptions, and satellite and i

title: (GCA-5 with Noblis): Earned Trust Framework for Ad Hoc Multi-Agent Autonomous Machine Team Orchestration
description: Our partnership between ASTRA* (GSFC) and Noblis will integrate capabilities from both entities to form the first integrated onboard AI system for spaceflight capable of completely autonomous multi-asset orchestration and analysis of environmental characteristics, hazards, risk management, and onboard decision-making in service of mission objectives. Our combined capability will be necessary for a sustained human presence on Mars or the Moon, and is targeted for several upcoming launch and propo

title: (GCA-4 with Noblis): Knowledge Mesh for Open Discovery and Access of Geospatial and Natural Language Data
description: This project aims to establish a semantically-integrated Knowledge Mesh (KM) to unify NASA’s space weather, heliophysics, and textual data for advanced discovery and analysis. This IRAD will focus on two solar missions, Solar Orbiter and Solar Dynamics Observatory (SDO) to map phenomena to observables such as solar radiation to extreme ultraviolet irradiance in the EVE dataset. In FY25, the foundational mesh architecture will be built within Noblis secure environment, including use case definiti

title: Dual-Wavelength Small All-range Lidar for Navigation and Water Ice Detection
description: We propose to improve our Small All-range LIdar (SALI) to enable water ice mapping and other minor modifications to extend its measurement capability to support NASA’s Moon-to-Mars program. The new lidar will be capable of topographic mapping, autonomous navigation and control, and on- and off-line differential absorption measurement for surface water ice detection. We will also investigate options to further reduce the instrument cost for new lunar orbital and landing mission opportunities.

title: In-Situ Ice Plume and Dust Detector Validation for Moon to Mars
description: This project aims to mature the in-house Exploration Payload Particle Ice Counter (EPPIC) from TRL 4 to TRL 5 by testing its ability to characterize dust particles and plumes traveling at 0.1-2000 m/s at conditions bracketing Moon and Mars environments, generated using the GSFC's Particle Impact and Plume Simulator (PIPS). EPPIC quantifies particle flux, size distribution, and dynamics, directly supporting Moon-to-Mars   (M2M) objectives LPS-2LM and AS-1LM [1] and indirectly supporting architect

title: autoNGC CAPSTONE Flight Experiment Completion
description: The Goddard-developed autonomy flight software, autoNGC, is scheduled for flight testing on the Cislunar Autonomous Positioning System Technology Operations and Navigation Experiment (CAPSTONE) extended mission during fiscal year 2026. The flight test will advance autoNGC to TRL 7 for autonomous onboard navigation and guidance capabilities in the cislunar environment, with particular emphasis on the lunar Gateway near-rectilinear halo orbit (NRHO). A successful flight test would position autoNGC
technologyArea: Other Communications, Navigation, and Orbital Debris Tracking and Characterization Systems

title: Readout Development for the HEPSTER Instrument to fill a Critical SWx Measurement Gap
description: The High Energy Proton SSD Telescope and chErenkov Radiator (HEPSTER) is a space weather instrument capable of measuring solar energetic particles (SEPs) in the unique energy range of ~10 – 500 MeV currently under development by the GSFC Code 672 Energetic Particle Laboratory (EPL).  The unique capabilities of HEPSTER have garnered significant interest from NASA HQ, NOAA (for the Sol-B mission), the Korean Space Agency, and OGA defense agencies. The largest remaining task in the development of H

title: Cloud-based XR for Rapid EXperience
description: This proposal seeks to provide an enterprise-wide, zero-configuration platform that enables collaborative 3D visualization of facilities, 3D models, and point clouds using tethered or standalone AR/VR (Augmented Reality/Virtual Reality) headsets or desktop interfaces through Mission Cloud Platform (MCP). The proposal is a partnership between the GSFC AR/VR Research & Development Lab and Noblis to create an end-to-end, open-source platform that democratizes access to advanced 3D visualization and

title: A Miniature INMS for VLEO satellites
description: The goal of this proposal is to optimize the Heliophysics Science Division’s miniature Ion-Neutral Mass Spectrometer (mini-INMS) instrument for VLEO applications including sensitivity, atomic oxygen resistance and low SWaP. The effort will support a VLEO technology demonstration mission in 2027

title: Multiple Reflection Time-of-Flight Mass Spectrometer
description: We propose to advance early development of a class of miniature, high resolution, time-of-flight mass spectrometers (TOF-MS) with the potential to realize measurement capabilities far beyond the current state of the art, and to position GSFC for multiple relevant robotic and crewed planetary missions (Mars, Moon, and beyond). The Multiple Reflection TOF (MRTOF) mass spectrometer offers transformational specifications including (1) extremely high mass resolution (m/m) exceeding 105 (FWHM) to dis

title: Compact Polarized Limb Sounder with Optimal Sub-millimeter to THz Channels for Mesosphere and Lower Thermosphere (MLT) Temperature, Wind and Atomic/Molecular Density Sounding
description: This project proposes to develop the foundational science and instrument parameters for a dual-use instrument concept - Compact Polarized Limb Sounder (CPLS) - to address the critical needs of atmospheric measurements in the Earth’s Mesosphere and Lower Thermosphere (MLT) and in future planetary atmosphere exploration (e.g., Mars). The envisioned CPLS can provide simultaneous, high-accuracy profiles of temperature, Doppler wind, and atmospheric density (e.g., atomic and molecular oxygen) soundin
technologyArea: Microwave, Millimeter Waves, and Submillimeter Waves

title: CLAViUS Mechanical Active Heat Switch for Lunar Night Survival
description: CLAViUS (Conduction-based Lunar Active Variable Universal Heat Switch) is a mechanically activated heat switch that directly addresses NASA’s number one civil space technology shortfall by enabling lunar surface payloads to survive and operate through the 14-day lunar night. The system mechanically engages to enhance heat rejection during the lunar day and disengages to prevent heat loss during the lunar night, aiming to achieve a turndown ratio ≥1250:1 in a 1U volume. This IRAD will advance CLA

title: Redesign of EVA Quick Disconnects for Exploration ATCS
description: Design new operator and fluid interface mechanisms to ensure user-friendly and dust-tolerant QDs for Artemis and beyond, increasing stakeholder confidence in critical hardware.​ (TRL-5 at completion o Year 2)

title: MLI Performance at Intermediate to Rough Vacuum Levels
description: This proposal aims to investigate the performance of multilayer insula􀆟on (MLI) at intermediate to rough vacuum levels, expanding beyond the conventional focus on high vacuum environments. While MLI is widely used for thermal insulation in cryogenic applications, most research has been conducted under high vacuum conditions where performance is optimal. However, many operational environments such as launch vehicles, spacecraft, and industrial cryogenics, often experience far less than ideal vacu

title: Dam Mars Study: Large Scale ISRU Propellant Production
description: Investigate the feasibility and risks of producing ISRU propellant for large-scale Mars ascent vehicles using hydroelectric power from the Martian ice caps. ​ For this concept, flow channels will be created with components printed from regolith and fluid from sub-glacial lakes will flow thru printed turbines to produce the required power output.​
technologyArea: Resource Processing for Production of Mission Consumables
partners: The University of Texas at Austin

title: CLIDE Camera
description: The CLIDE camera is needed by the CubeSpark mission to detect small, dim electrical discharges that frequent intense thunderstorms and often elude current global lightning mappers. CubeSpark, which is being developed by NASA and Los Alamos National Lab, will use a constellation of small satellites to autonomously detect lightning and map it in 3-D for the first time globally. The CLIDE camera will utilize a new CMOS image sensor (CIS) to acquire higher resolution images and be packaged in a smal

title: Lunar and Planetary Plume Surface Interaction Environmental Test Rig
description: This project proposes to adapt an existing plasma spray facility to serve as a controllable testbed for evaluating erosion of candidate surface materials under simulated lunar and Martian plume-surface interaction (PSI) conditions. By operating under rough vacuum and integrating regolith simulant injection, the system will simulate high-velocity particle impingement from lander plumes. The effort will be paired with peridynamic computational modeling performed by researchers at Virginia Commonwe

title: Transonic Uncertainty Modeling By Limited Expansion of Aerodynamics
description: This project will create a low-cost but accurate transonic aerodynamic model for EDL flight missions by expanding existing aerodatabases to include static forces and moments at high angles of attack and applying oscillatory forcing to bound tumbling behavior. The main advantage over capturing transonic tumbling dynamics through expensive forced oscillation testing in a transonic tunnel facility is orders of magnitude in cost savings and access to an engineering model for low-cost NASA projects a

title: Turbomachinery Liquefaction Demonstration with a Recuperator
description: Commercial Space Partners are shifting focus to Mars Surface Habitation and are interested in the development of Mars Propellant Production. The Martian atmosphere consists of 95% CO2 and 5% inert gas. CO2 can be collected and reacted with hydrogen (H2) to form CH4. The Sabatier Reaction, an ECLSS technology, has historically utilized H2 (a byproduct from water electrolysis used for oxygen generation) and metabolic CO2 to produce CH4 and additional water to be used in a closed-loop ECLSS water s

title: Construction Implements for In-Situ resource utilization Construction
description: Proposal seeks to further KSC's extreaterrestrial compaction for ISRU construction capabilities that meet potential partner needs and expands applicability to multiple programs. PI will partner with industry to advance compaction capabilities to TRL 6.

title: AI- and Modeling-Optimized Acoustic Mufflers for Next-Generation Spacesuits and Spaceflight
description: Develops AI-optimized acoustic mufflers for spacesuits, targeting noise from the Portable Life Support System (PLSS). Designs are tailored to PLSS constraints, simulated thru FEA&CFD, 3D-printed, and tested. The framework extends to HLS, Gateway, habitats and rovers, enhancing spaceflight acoustic performance. In addiiton, the framework/workflow could apply to other NASA areas.​
technologyArea: Environmental Control and Life Support Systems and Habitation Systems

title: SHM Edge Computing Test Bed for Expediting Technology Transition
description: “Edge nodes” that collect sensor data and perform analysis at the point of acquisition are a key to enabling real-time health monitoring of space vehicles and systems with minimal additional data transmission and flight computer workload.  This proposal is for development of a modular, customizable edge node test bed to aid in transitioning lab-based sensing methods to space-based applications.  A demonstration using  ultrasonic data will demonstrate acquisition, preprocessing, and event classif

title: Study of mechanical and oxidative impacts of PTFE soft goods in valve assemblies used for NTO sampling
description: Proposal will produce material characterization data of PTFE seals after NTO exposure to inform decisions for reuse of current sampling equipment and critical data for future fabrication assessments. The proposal will evaluate mechanical stress, polymeric degradation, physical degradation, and long term offgassing.
technologyArea: Other Ground, Testing, and Surface Systems

title: Biomass Automated Technology Harvester
description: Proposal will develop an automated biomass harvester to collect and process algae as a valuable Bioregenerative Life Support System (BLiSS) and biomanufacturing feedstock. Establish an effective and efficient method for harvesting microalgae biomass in space.
technologyArea: Human Health, Life Support, and Habitation Systems

title: VESPEr: Vapor Experiment for Space and Planetary Exploration
description: VESPEr is a low-SWaP instrument for atmospheric vapor measurements on Entry, Descent and Landing (EDL) missions to Mars and other planets. VESPEr leverages the design of the Contamination Monitoring Package (CMP) of the SAGE III/ISS instrument operating in orbit since 2017. Prior efforts revealed the need to experimentally demonstrate the instrument’s performance under various planetary atmospheric conditions. Thus, the next step is to test VESPEr in a simulation chamber, which will be construct

title: Adaptable Radiometric Measurement System
description: Development of Adaptable Radiometric Measurement System (ARMS) to conduct no-contact high temperature measurement >2600F

title: Smart and Efficient Filters for Moon to Mars
description: Develop a  “smart” spacecraft air filter system is proposed to meet the demands of remote missions to the Moon and Mars that will extend filter life, reduce or eliminate manual maintenance and the number of onboard spare filters.
technologyArea: Environmental Control and Life Support Systems and Habitation Systems

title: FUN4D: A New Paradigm for Time-Dependent Simulations
description: We propose a radically new methodology for time-dependent flow simulations in four-dimensional (4D) space-time domains. Our methodology fully couples space and time, effectively treating time as a fourth spatial dimension. The space-time simulations will be carried out on adapted, 4D unstructured grids for maximum efficiency and flexibility. Our method will extend NASA’s state-of-the-art CFD solver FUN3D (https://fun3d.larc.nasa.gov) to 4D, thereby creating FUN4D, in order to effectively use mod
technologyArea: Software, Modeling, Simulation, and Information Processing

title: Extreme Condition Capable Sensors (2nd year: $200k)
description: Conventional sensors like pressure transducers, thermocouples, RTD’s, etc. struggle to survive in extreme temperature or vibration environments. This IRAD project would study and ultimately propose sensors capable of withstanding environments such as combustion chambers. This project could explore methods such as special coatings for sensors to optical and/or acoustical sensors. This technology could be a significant advancement that is particularly beneficial for high-energy applications like r

title: Improved Ignition for Low-Cost, Non-Toxic Hybrid Propulsion Systems
description: Install and test several advanced ignition methods for a small Nitrous Oxide Hybrid de-orbit propulsion system built at JSC.​

title: Surviving Lunar Night with Solid State Battery Systems
description: We propose a novel energy storage strategy for lunar night survival by using solid-state battery (SSB) systems. In comparison to conventional Li ion batteries with liquid electrolytes that freeze at ultralow temperatures, solid electrolytes in SSBs are non-flammable and never freeze even if cooling to extreme cold, thus significantly improve the reliability of energy storage systems without excessive thermal and safety protection systems. Proposed study will focus on cost effective sodium-based

title: Au Release Layer for High-Angular Resolution Full Shell X-ray Optics
description: We propose ~150k in procurements to enable the development of an Au release layer technology for replicated full shell X-ray optics. Current fabrication processes result in optic circularity errors that are likely dominated by release-induced distortions. The Au release layer could reduce these errors, improving optical figure and advancing optical performance to the sub-arcsecond level.   The optimized gold release layer has been applied to small-scale mandrels from which a NiCo replicated shel

title: Simulated Inspection Mechanics for Artificial Intelligence Defect Detection
description: In this study, we propose to use X-Ray simulation to generate synthetic training data for artificial intelligence (AI) defect detection, applied to aerospace additively manufactured (AM) metal components. The team has extensive experience with nondestructive evaluation, metallic AM, X-Ray simulation, AI / machine learning, and data science. We intend to perform head-to-head comparisons of leading AI defect detection algorithms trained with simulated data, and demonstrate performance for critical

title: Optimized Hydrogen Reliquefication System (2nd year: TBD)
description: This proposal seeks to develop an advanced Liquid Hydrogen (LH) reliquefication system to enhance long-term storage capabilities in aerospace and energy sectors. The system will leverage cryogenic technology to efficiently re-liquefy vaporized hydrogen, minimizing boil-off losses and enabling sustainable hydrogen management. This project aims to improve system performance, reliability, and scalability for use in hydrogen storage for propulsion systems, fuel cells, energy applications, etc. The e

title: AI-Driven Sensor Configuration & Anomoly Detection
description: Develop an AI-driven configuration assistant that suggests configurations to train a clustering model (e.g., k-means) and recommends optimal parameters (gain, filter, scaling equations) based on sensor type and measurement goals. Also that validates inputs by employing an anomaly detection model (e.g., isolation forest) to flag unusual parameter combinations in real-time, alerting users to potential errors.  The end goal is to seamlessly integrate both features into the MVVM architecture, levera

title: Sequencing Manifold : Biological implementation in SAMMS ConOps, Part II
description: Proposal will advance the the development of Spaceflight Automated Multigenerational Microbial Sequencer (SAMMS) to improve genome resolution. Mature plant DNA processing to develop instruments for in-situ processing during spaceflight.

title: Water vapor Infrared Sunlight Sounder with Photonic integrated circuits
description: Sounders measure absorption lines of atmospheric gases and use this to calculate the vertical concentration of those gases and create a 3D image of gas distribution. Typical sounders have limited vertical resolution and high uncertainty due to measuring only a single absorption feature. We propose to develop an improved sounder design using photonic integrated circuits (PICs) ideal for sounding of water vapor, a major driver of weather that is prioritized in the Earth Science Decadal Survey. The

title: Development of a Sensitive, Hand-held, Real-time Formaldehyde Monitor for Spaceflight Operations
description: Formaldehyde (HCHO) exposures for crew members are closely monitored in human spaceflight, as the acceptable limit is low. Currently-planned trace contaminant monitors for Exploration missions cannot meet HCHO sensitivity requirements, leaving the programs with limited insight into this compound, particularly for post-quiescence. This project aims to develop a small, portable, real-time HCHO monitor with high sensitivity for in-flight and ground-based operations with minimal crew maintenance req
technologyArea: Air, Water, Microbial, and Acoustic Sensors

title: Small-scale Cmd & Ctrl Remote End to End Mission Support System
description: Create an airworthy ground control capability that accommodates and easily integrates a range of platforms of various size, complexity, and criticality that is compatible with Armstrong and Edwards Air force Base range safety. Specifically, the development of a NASA GCS software that is rapidly  configurable to each projects needs. This development will not only enhance the ability to fly but will support projects through control law analysis and development of further one-of-a-kind technologica

title: Generic Granular Feedstock Beneficiation System for Planetary Surface Construction
description: Develop process to produce ready-for-use feedstocks for surface infra-structure (dirtwork and additive manufacturing), from any available regolith input

title: Integrated Magnetohydrodynamic Propulsion Unit for Launch and Space energy
description: As the world’s premier propulsion department, NASA is committed to advancing next-generation rocket engine technologies to improve efficiency, reliability, and sustainability in space exploration. The IMPULSE project aligns with NASA’s strategic goals by pioneering an innovative propulsion system that replaces conventional turbomachinery with a magnetohydrodynamic (MHD) generator-driven electric pump. By leveraging MHD principles, this approach enhances mission adaptability while reducing comple

title: TrajGPT: Beyond Low Earth Orbit Trajectory Generation via Machine Learning
description: Ensuring a deep space crew can arrive at their destination at the appropriate time and return safely requires detailed planning. Current design methods involve generating millions of trajectories and analyzing countless permutations of variables such as launch date and time, propellant budgets, and abort options (too complex to be done onboard in real-time).  Project will utilize machine learning to investigate methods which could replace current, brute-force physics-based modeling with big data

title: Advanced Structural Systems Material Characterization to Establish NASA Database
description: Inflatable softgoods and composite primary structures provide significant volume/mass benefits and can increase capabilities when implemented within surface/in-space habitation and logistics elements, transportation, and mobility systems relative to current state of the art metallic options.  The mechanical behavior of these advanced material systems has only been studied at a component level in detail, with few larger-scale module tests conducted that include the effects of fabrication and biax
technologyArea: Materials, Structures, Mechanical Systems, and Manufacturing

title: Space-based Image Matching
description: Develops an image matching algorithm tailor-made to space-based applications, improving capabilities in remote sensing, terrain-relative navigation, and multimodal data fusion. This single model can be applied to tasks across JSC to support mission planning, operations, and science. ​
technologyArea: Sensing and Perception for Autonomous Systems

title: The design of fibrous insulation to minimize radiative heat transfer for TPS
description: This project proposes to develop and validate a physics-based capability to predict radiative heat transfer in fibrous insulations for thermal protection systems. Current design approaches utilize semi-empirical relationships based on measured data to define radiative heat transfer performance. The proposed effort will solve the radiation transport equation and predict thermal performance based on fiber morphology and optical properties. The project will characterize and study the performance of
partners: Air Force Office of Scientific Research

title: Technology Maturation Pod
description: Develop common 19" and 9.5" size instrumentation rack for existing travel pod along with common interface for power, data, and GPS antenna connection. Flight test updated pod. Develop user guide for researchers to be able to utilize pod.

title: Full-Scale Trash-to-Gas System Demonstration
description: Proposal seeks to manufacture, build, and test a full-scale, flight-forward Trash-to-Gas system for a ground demonstration. Year 1 will be designing of a full scale TtG prototype. Year 2 will be the manufacutring of the prototype for demonstration.

title: Design of a Spaceflight Oxygen Compression System
description: Near-Earth operations allow NASA to leverage terrestrial capabilities to enable space activities via resupply. High-pressure oxygen (HPO2) for extra-vehicular activities (EVAs), where spacesuits use a 100% oxygen environment, are currently dependent on terrestrial capabilities. HPO2 is provided to ISS via routine resupply of pre-pressurized tanks, but this system poses a prohibitively high upmass for long-duration missions given its tank-to-oxygen mass ratio of 3:2. Projects at MSFC have previou
technologyArea: Human Health, Life Support, and Habitation Systems

title: Mission Systems Research T-34
description: To develop a mission concept for the T-34 and the necessary flight systems to enable rapid, low-cost, repeatable access to a spectrum of flight environments for the evaluation of novel sensors, pilot displays, novel actuators, and algorithms. Start open loop with control loop closed by the pilot based on displays and full RFCS closed loop capability in out years.

title: Advancement of an Actuated Upper Extremity Offload System for Integration into ARGOS
description: Smart, powered wearable system that will integrate with spacesuits during ARGOS/AX3S lunar/Martian surface tests to accurately offload the user’s upper extremities. This system would have a MAJOR IMPACT on suited ARGOS runs (both user fatigue and accuracy of metabolic data) and ENABLE accurate EVA design. IRAD funding will support will culmination with ARGOS human-in-the-loop assessments of the final version, and delivery of the final design and findings.​
technologyArea: Ground Analogs for Space and Surface Systems

title: Flowmeter Calibration Capability (2nd year: $250k)
description: There are currently very few vendors capable of empirically calibrating flow meters and orifices at pressures needed to match Reynolds number for flow conditions generally required in rocket propulsion testing. NASA does not currently have this capability. Agency & commercial test and flight programs regularly require the use of calibrated flow components, often surpassing the capabilities of current existing facilities. This setup will eliminate the agency’s dependence on the limited existing f

title: Glenn Regolith Composites for Sustainable Lunar Operations and Presence in Extreme Environments
description: Developing a fiber reinforced lunar regolith-based geopolymer composite that is light weight, fire-, heat- and radiation-resistant with high strength utilizing 90% in-situ resources (ISRU) and minimum amount of earth sourced materials.
technologyArea: In Situ Manufacturing, Maintenance, and Repair

title: Risk-Bounded, Goal Driven AI for Human Planetary Exploration
description: This risk-bounded, goal-driven approach builds off academic research on autonomous robotic exploration. For instance, autonomous underwater vehicles have used similar approaches onboard to develop plans, avoid obstacles, and complete science tasks.​ Recognizing that surface operations on the Moon and Mars necessitates consideration of many sources of risk, e.g., life support, crater proximity, shadows, slope, comm. coverage, etc., there is a need to model risk and quantify its impact. The tool e

title: Enabling Niobium-based Alloy and Coating Technologies
description: Design and benchmark novel oxygen-tolerant additively manufactured (AM) Nb-based alloys with advanced environmental barrier coatings (EBCs)

title: X-Ray Fluorescence Rated for Flight
description: Proposal will start the development of a flight-rated X-Ray Fluorescence (XRF) spectrometer as an in-situ analytical tool for Artemis III/IV and a path to Mars application. The XRF spectrometer will be used in a a study to demonstrate instrument sensitivity and operation requirements.

title: New 2 µm Capabilities for Next Generation Spaceborne Lidar Measurements of Terrestrial Resources and Hazards
description: Lidar instruments are key sensors for measuring terrestrial resources like forests, water, and agricultural lands, as well as hazards such as hurricanes, wildfires, and volcanoes. However, most lidar instruments traditionally operate in the visible or near infrared. This proposal advances subsystem technologies, specifically within the 2 µm wavelength regime, that can be employed in next generation surface topography and vegetation (STV), cloud-aerosol, and atmospheric wind lidars.

title: Laser Embedded Drill Bits for Planetary and Terrestrial Exploration
description: We are partnering with Blue Origin’s Honeybee Robotics to develop exploration drills with embedded laser technology that can identify material in real time while operating below the surface. For planetary exploration, this capability will speed up the discovery of water, minerals, and potential biosignatures on the Moon and Mars. On Earth, this will enable more precise discovery of mining sites, offering real-world payoff as demand for rare earth materials continues to grow rapidly.

title: Developing Miniaturized Wind and Weather Lidar for Mars Exploration and Atmospheric Science
description: This proposed work will demonstrate and mature new technologies needed for two types of atmospheric lidar for Mars. The first is a wind lidar (Mini-MARLI) for Mars orbit that makes wind profile measurements of Mars from the surface to 50 km altitude for the entire globe. The other is surface-based lidar (the Laser Atmospheric Profiling System, LAPS) which will measure real-time vertical profiles of winds, water vapor, dust, and aerosols from the surface to 10 km as shown in Figure 1. Both instru

title: ARIA: AI-Enhanced Requirements and Integration Assistant Building an LLM Hybrid Reasoner as a Prototype System Engineering
description: ARIA represents a transformative approach to NASA's Systems Requirements Review (SRR) process by developing an AI-enhanced copilot that transitions from document-driven to architecture-driven milestone reviews. The system leverages Large Language Models (LLMs), Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE), and semantic reasoning to provide immediate, comprehensive responses to SRR questions while maintaining rigorous standards. ARIA will integrate with MagicDraw MBSE tools to create a knowledge base t

title: Stitched Infused Thermoset Composite Hatch/window Receptacle
description: Stitched Infused Thermoset Composite Hatch/window Receptacle (StITCHR) will design, analyze, manufacture, and test a stitched composite pressurized cabin window receptacle. The activity will explore developing technologies pertinent to stitching of composite preforms (fastener encapsulating stitching) and serve as a design cycle demonstration of stitched composites integrated into pressurized volumes.

title: BioMole Preparation (PowerBlade)
description: As NASA progresses to longer, farther, and more complex destinations, providing our crew members with a safe and healthy environment is essential to mission success. Our current process for microbial surface sampling includes returning samples to be processed and analyzed on the ground. Martian missions will not include sample return, so we must ensure the crew can handle and analyze samples within their habitat. The Exploration Medical Integrated Product Team (XMIPT), in conjunction with the
technologyArea: Human Health, Life Support, and Habitation Systems

title: In Situ Lab Analysis
description: Exploration missions will feature austere habitable environments, volume and mass constraints, limited to no resupply, and communication delays that will impose challenges to evaluating and maintaining crew health and performance. Due to increased exposure to the hazards of human space flight and the lack of evacuation capabilities, exploration missions will demand increasingly detailed coordination of medical care between crewmembers and care providers despite the decreasing availability of
technologyArea: Human Health, Life Support, and Habitation Systems

title: Crew Connectedness Adv Comm Platform
description: Exploration missions will feature austere habitable environments, small volumes, mass constraints, limitations in infrastructure (such as bandwidth limitations) and communications delay that will impose challenges to evaluating and maintaining crew's behavioral health and performance.  Future space exploration, including missions to Mars, will include a significant communication delay between astronauts and those on Earth (e.g., friends and family, medical and psychological support providers,
technologyArea: Human Health, Life Support, and Habitation Systems
partners: U.S. Air Force 59th Medical Wing of 711th Human Performance Wing

title: Portable ECG Device
description: As NASA prepares for longer duration missions that will take us farther than ever before, we must prioritize our most valuable spaceflight asset: humans. Future Martian missions present a new set of challenges, including communication delays that could be harmful if a crew member faced a medical situation and needed care. While we must consider humans first, other factors such as vehicle space, consumables, and ease of use play a role in the medical hardware we send with our crews. This proje
technologyArea: Human Health, Life Support, and Habitation Systems

title: Universal Waste Management System
description:
The objective of the Universal Waste Management System (UWMS) project is to develop a toilet system that can be used across multiple future crewed vehicles and habitats. The ISS Toilet Project effort will result in a toilet with reduced mass and volume that provides increased crew comfort and performance. A key feature of the UWMS is the urine pretreatment dose pump/pretreatment quality indication device which enables water recovery from urine.

The Toilet will first serve as an ISS tech
partners: Hamilton Sundstrand Space Systems International Inc

title: Martian Surface Environment Simulation Capability
description: We are asking for ~$140K for materials and labor to prototype the thermal control system needed to simulate the Martian atmosphere in V20. To support the Martian atmosphere capability of V20, a conceptual design to allow for better thermal control has been developed for one of the smaller TVAC chambers. In addition to matching the pressure and composition of the Martian Atmosphere, it is necessary to achieve better temperature control for Martian Atmosphere testing. Temperature control with the

title: Pharmacy
description: Exploration missions beyond low Earth orbit will experience significant challenges in crew medical care. Among these challenges are the exposure of provided medications to vacuum conditions. The impacts of environmental exposures of vacuum on medications and packaging are not fully characterized. Vehicles supporting the Artemis missions will have dedicated, vehicle-specific medical kits as well as crew medical accessory kits. Although most of the kits will be stored in a pressurized, climate-

title: Pharmacy Shelf-Life
description: Exploration missions beyond low Earth orbit will experience significant challenges in crew medical care. Among these challenges are limited or no resupply missions to replenish medication inventory. Ensuring medication safety and efficacy will buy down medical risk. For anticipated medications planned for inclusion in future exploration medical systems, the time from packaging to use will exceed manufacturer-reported shelf-life. This poses a risk that medications may be ineffective when used.
partners: U.S. Air Force 59th Medical Wing of 711th Human Performance Wing

title: Clinical Decision Support
description: The Clinical Decision Support (CDS) Project is a NASA initiative to enable safe, autonomous medical care for crews on future Lunar Base, Mars transit, and Mars surface missions, where communication delays and blackouts will preclude real-time guidance from Earth. Under these conditions, astronauts must independently detect, diagnose, and treat medical events, even when no physician is onboard and crew medical training time is limited. This project integrates clinical, human factors, and softw
technologyArea: Human Health, Life Support, and Habitation Systems

title: Frozen Return Samples to Earth
description: FROSTE is focused on providing the Science community with mobile deep-cryogenic stowage of samples collected on the lunar or Martian surfaces and environmentally controlled accommodation back to Earth for scientific research. One of the Moon 2 Mars program objectives is to satisfy the Lunar/Planetary Science goals that address high-priority planetary science questions. These goals are best addressed by on-site human explorers on and around the Moon and Mars, facilitated by surface and orbitin
partners: The University of Texas at El Paso

title: Bioregenerative Life Support Systems
description: For Artemis missions exceeding 120 days, NASA astronauts will need a completely regenerative life support system in Moon Base habitation modules. Traditional physical-chemical life support systems have over 40 years of use aboard International Space Station (ISS) and are ideal for short duration missions where hardware size and volume aren’t available. However, these systems are not regenerable, as constant resupply is required to maintain ISS through the resupply of nitrogen and oxygen (NORS
technologyArea: Human Health, Life Support, and Habitation Systems

title: autoNGC CAPSTONE Flight Experiment Completion
description: The NASA/GSFC-developed autonomy flight software, autoNGC (autonomous Navigation, Guidance and Control), is scheduled for flight testing on the Cislunar Autonomous Positioning System Technology Operations and Navigation Experiment (CAPSTONE) extended mission during fiscal year 2026. The flight test will advance autoNGC to TRL 7 for autonomous onboard navigation and guidance capabilities in the cislunar environment, with particular emphasis on the lunar Gateway near-rectilinear halo orbit (NRH

title: Tandem Ion Mass Spectrometer (TIMS): High Efficiency Multi-Stop Design
description: Build a prototype of our Tandem Ion Mass Spectrometer (TIMS), that can separate the mass and charge of atomic ions and can separate atomic and molecular ions of similar mass-per-charge ratio (M/Q). This prototype TIMS currently has the opportunity to fly on a sounding rocket in FY25 as a technology demonstration instrument. One of the main innovative aspects of TIMS is the capability of a really low noise floor, allowing for the detection of minor ion species in a variety of plasma environmen

title: S-Band Augmented Forward Service (AFS) Receiver for Lunar Navigation
description: Lunar users, whether on the surface or in orbit, need access to PNT services to determine their position, execute maneuvers and maintain accurate time to meet mission objectives. Space assets typically receive PNT services through point-to-point links with Earth ground stations or Relay satellites using ranging, Doppler tracking and messaging schemes. However, to enable users to receive GPS-like global PNT services independent of dedicated communications links, the Lunar Augmented Navigation

title: Deci-Hz Interferometric Space Antenna
description: Gravitational-wave (GW) astrophysics has begun in earnest with the early successes of LIGO, and the substantial progress of the LISA mission toward launch in the 2030s. The unexplored “Deci-Hz” (dHz) band lies in between LISA and LIGO, containing guaranteed early warning of multi-messenger LIGO stellar origin compact binaries of black holes (SOBH) and neutron stars (BNS), potentially exciting additional sources like double-white dwarf systems merging to cause Type Ia supernovae, intermediate

title: UV Contrast Performance and Yield Improvement of the NGMSA for Use on HWO
description: We will be following a plan of work that follows on from FY23 IRAD work to measure the contrast performance of next generation micro shutter arrays (NGMSAs), as well as an investigation of electrostatic repeatability issues associated with shutter open/close and manufacture shorts in the silicon structure. Our work will demonstrate not only the performance of the device but the control and manufacturability of the shutters and their blades as well as control of the light shield structure to p

title: Assessing Plant Canopy Energy Balance, Evapotranspiration, and Vegetation Stress with 3D Surface Temperatures Derived from New G-LiHT TIR Imagery
description: The drivers and responses of vegetation three-dimensional structure and functioning to ongoing global change, land-use, and altered disturbance regimes is not well understood, but play an important role in regulating land-atmosphere interactions and ecosystem-atmosphere exchange of CO2, CH4, heat and water vapor. A critical uncertainty relates to the complex interplay between vegetation structure and the emergent functioning and surface energy balance over space and time. To address this crit
technologyArea: Software, Modeling, Simulation, and Information Processing

title: Testing and Extension of Onboard Maneuver Planning Algorithms
description: The goal of this IRAD is to develop a flight software (FSW) implementation of a maneuver planning algorithm to include in Goddard’s software for autonomous navigation, guidance, and control, called autoNGC. The primary focus of this early-stage innovation is in-house development of maneuver planning FSW, and a secondary focus is cooperating with other NASA centers to assess the potential for incorporating their maneuver planning FSW tools within autoNGC. Developing an autonomous maneuver plan

title: Evaluating COTS Hardware for Gasless Sounding Rocket Payload Attitude Control
description: Gas released by current sounding rocket payload attitude control systems (ACS) has the potential to interfere with some types of science instruments. Previous IRAD work was done to design a gasless reaction wheel ACS that would work for a sounding rocket payload, during which it was determined that commercial off the shelf (COTS) reaction wheels would not be well suited for a full payload due to the larger moments of inertia (MoI) and limited flight durations of sounding rocket payloads. Howe
technologyArea: Dynamics Analysis, Modeling, and Simulation Tools

title: Text-to-Spaceship: Revolutionizing Mission Development through AI and Evolved Systems
description: This proposal aims to revolutionize NASA Goddard's mission development by infusing AI and computational design to accelerate development cycles and realize game-changing performance improvements. It will establish a comprehensive Text-to-Spaceship framework for integrating AI into all aspects of mission development, facilitating the transition to AI-enabled automation with human collaboration. Evolved Systems, a key component of the Text-to-Spaceship framework, will automate the design of com
technologyArea: Mission Architecture, Systems Analysis, and Concept Development

title: cFS Multithread Parallelization Capability
description: This project proposes to update the cFS Operating System Abstraction Layer (OSAL) to add SMP capabilities for use with each of the operating systems supported by cFS: Linux, RTEMS, and VxWorks. While cFS was originally created for single-core systems, there’s growing interest in multi-core usage and NASA has started to utilize multi-core processors for its space missions such as the GR712 (2 cores) used in Plankton, Aerosol, Cloud, ocean Ecosystem’s (PACE) Modular Unified Space Technology Avi

title: Investigation of Data Augmentation Techniques for Machine Learning Performance on Mass Spectrometry Data
description: Mass spectrometers are powerful instruments that aim to identify unknown compounds via their molecular weight, as well as perform quantitative analysis, and have been essential to space missions since the 1970s through today (e.g., JUICE, Dragonfly, Europa Clipper). As identified in the Planetary Decadal Survey, missions targeting remote planetary bodies will face limited data rates and volumes and will require onboard science autonomy to optimize science ret
technologyArea: Software, Modeling, Simulation, and Information Processing

title: A real-time seismic detection technology for the Moon and beyond
description: The objective of this effort is to develop a real-time seismic detection technology using machine learning signal change detection for both lander-side decision-making for telemetry prioritization and rapid assessment of incoming streamed data on Earth. The technology (CORGI, short for COntinous Realtime monitorinG Infrastructure) will be modular, able to integrate diverse detection algorithms, instrument typ
technologyArea: Software, Modeling, Simulation, and Information Processing

title: Fabrication and Evaluation for the Rad-Hard Housekeeping Slave Node (RH-HKSN) ASIC for Power System Electronics
description: This project aims to aggressively reduce the size of the Power System Electronics (PSE), low-voltage telemetry, and housekeeping (HK) electronics. The goal is to combine multiple discrete analog, digital, and mixed-signal devices into a single smart, radiation hardened, system-on-a-chip (SOC) chip solution. Ultimately this effort seeks to produce a flight worthy rad-hard device that will replace several discrete components in current and future PSE designs thus reducing the size, mass, power,

title: Determining Radiation Hardness of Novel Visible-Blind Photodetectors for UV Astrophysics Applications
description: GSFC and industry partners are currently pursuing the development of UV-sensitive, visible-blind SiC and AlGaN detectors, which are ideal for UV astrophysics applications, especially broadband near-UV imaging (200-300 nm, e.g., Swift/UVOT, ULTRASAT, UVEX). We will determine whether these UV detectors are sufficiently radiation-hard for space-based applications by carrying out deep irradiation gamma-ray and proton testing.

title: Thin film Fabry–Pérot cavity for enhancing optical interaction with quantum dot based filters
description: To augment the performance of quantm dot (QD) spectrometers, a novel spectrometry technology already under development at NASA GSFC with particular applications for planetary science, we propose integration with a light capturing thin film Fabry–Pérot cavity to increase optical interaction with QD filters. By adding partially reflective layers above and below the QD spectrometer array, light is forced to cycle within the layers, continuously interacting absorptive QD filters (Figure 1), impro

title: Long-Wave Infrared Upgrades for the CHARMS Refractometer
description: This project will extend the measurement capabilities of the Cryogenic High Accuracy Refraction Measuring System (CHARMS) facility into the long wave infrared (LWIR) spectral region. The CHARMS refractometer is one of the distinctives capabilities within the Optics Branch at GSFC. This refractometer was originally developed in the early 2000’s to provide highly accurate cryogenic index of refraction measurements of the various glass materials that were used in the Near-Infrared Camera

title: Volumetric 3D Mapping of Complex Dielectric Permittivity of Mars via Laboratory Multi-orientation Analysis of Mars Meteorites with Direct Implications for Future Mars Exploration, Part II
description: The goal of this effort is to develop and establish a new and unique, non-destructive methodology for the three-dimensional (3-D) characterization of the complex dielectric permittivity (CDP) of arbitrary shaped planetary rocks and Earth analog samples. Our work will set a path to produce the first world-class type facility for the 3-D “mapping” of the isotropic and anisotropic dielectric properties of a variety of rock and soil samples, relevant to Synthetic Apertur

title: Advancement of High-Resolution Gamma-ray Spectrometer
description: We are developing a high-resolution gamma-ray spectrometer for the purpose of studying solar flare gamma-ray line emission in the ~0.5-10 MeV range. We are employing a cutting edge CeBr3 scintillator which has high photoelectric effect cross sections in the MeV range read out by silicon photomultipliers. We will use the high-heritage readout electronics built for the BurstCube mission updated for CeBr3 signals. The primary effort will be to update the readout electronics and signal processing

title: Enable cloud liquid water path (CLWP) measurements with a broad-band digital system on HIWRAP
description: This project is to develop a radiometric capability on the High Altitude Wind and Rain Airborne Profiler (HIWRAP) radar while modernizing its digital system and simplifying its RF design. This work replaces the existing command and data handling (C&DH) system, digital receiver, power distribution unit, and intermediate frequency (IF) boxes with a single electronics box containing on-hand radar processing modules, a single-board computer, and some supporting electronics.
technologyArea: Microwave, Millimeter Waves, and Submillimeter Waves

title: Towards Dynamic Configuration for a Cloud Evolution Targeting Radar System
description: We explore a novel scanning cloud radar concept for observing the evolution of low-level clouds and convective initiation in a targeted manner. Results from high resolution cloud model simulations will be used to perform a trade study to define measurement requirements and help inform design specifications of radar system with a metasurface antenna for electronic steerable beam scanning in two dimensions. A key goal of the study is to identify enabling technology to develop a new measurement

title: Advancing computed tomography techniques for surface deployment on the Moon, Mars, asteroids, and comets
description: X-ray computed tomography (XCT) provides 3D visualization of the interior structure of planetary materials, such as rocks and ices. The proposed work will advance capabilities of XCT instruments to be deployed on landers/rovers. By increasing chemical/structural sensitivity through the use of X-ray filtering and hyperspectral imaging, the proposed work would have applications across Moon, Mars, asteroid, and comet missions.

title: Standardizing a Data and Power System for GSFC Long-Duration Balloon Flights
description: We propose to develop an optical detection technique that can be used to measure NO2 with a balloon-borne sonde (in a follow-on IRAD). This IRAD, we intend to see if this measurement works in a lab environment.

title: Using Earth-based Observations to Bootstrap Expected Signatures of Exoplanets
description: While multi-dimensional physical-chemical models of the upper atmospheres of Earth, Venus and Mars have been developed, they suffer from the many approximations that are related to the pure knowledge of network reaction and reaction rates ignited due to energetic particle precipitation. The major objective of the proposed research is to conduct a comprehensive and inter-disciplinary theory guided experimental study of Earth’s atmospheric chemistry irradiated by the energetic electrons and pro

title: 3D Atmospheric Formaldehyde Profiler
description: Formaldehyde (HCHO) has been a key target for NASA’s upper atmospheric and tropospheric chemistry research programs because of its role in various atmospheric oxidative processes involving greenhouse gases. Additionally, there has been recent interest in detecting HCHO in the Martian atmosphere as it serves as a marker for methane concentration. To this end, we propose to build and test a high-power 353 nm fiber laser transmitter based on large mode area fiber technology that will serve as an

title: Metamaterial Metaoptic Optical Frame Step-2 Proposal
description: This IRAD project focuses on developing a cutting-edge metamaterial metaoptic optical frame for advanced imaging and sensing applications. By leveraging the unique properties of metamaterials, the frame will control light propagation in unprecedented ways, enabling superior optical performance in terms of resolution, compactness, and wavelength range. The project aims to design and fabricate a lightweight, highly efficient optical system that integrates seamlessly with various devices, offeri

title: Implementing a hardware acceleration for the lunar gravity model
description: The objective of this IRAD is to develop, implement, test and verify the 150x150 lunar gravity model for orbit determination (OD) and prediction on the FPGA during the fiscal year 2025. The goal of this task is to meet the Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter (LRO) requirements for OD and prediction accuracy, which are 500 meters total position root mean squared (RMS) and 18 meters radial RMS for definitive ephemeris accuracy and predicted orbit accuracy less than 800 meters root sum squared (RSS) ov

title: Bi-Directional High Altitude Aircraft Atmospheric Lidar Instrument Performance Improvement
description: Stratospheric aerosols can have large effects on both the radiative balance of the planet and atmospheric composition. In this region of the atmosphere, aerosols are predominantly sulfate, much of them volcanic in origin. Analogously, explosive pyrocumulonimbus (pyroCb) convection from extreme midlatitude and boreal forest fire events can directly inject aerosols into the stratosphere. After injection, volcanic and pyroCb aerosols can persist for many months in the stratosphere; however, the

title: Space-Borne Ultra Low Noise Clock – High TRL Engineering module for VBLI telescope and High Precision Clock Enabled Astrophysics
description:
1.Development of an Ultra-high stability space qualified clock and clock synchronization system for science applications

2.System will service mission of Very Long Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) in ground-to-space or space-to-space configurations.

title: Antenna-Coupled MIR/FIR Detectors for High Sensitivity Applications
description: This is an effort to develop antenna-coupled detectors for mid- and far- infrared high sensitivity astrophysics applications. The effort will unite multiple relevant technology achievements to extend this detector technology to higher frequencies and more sensitive detectors.

title: A Microwave Land Surface Emissivity Model for the Hyperspectral Future
description: This project proposes the development of a surface emissivity model to be used in PBL investigations using new hyperspectral microwave technology, with further applications to precipitation via the GPM constellation and general atmospheric remote sensing in the microwave. An upcoming field campaign being led by GSFC will test such technology in anticipation of a pathfinder spaceborne mission to fly in three years with a goal of demonstrating atmospheric sounding in the planetary boundary laye

title: Advanced Langmuir Probe for Measurements in high atomic oxygen environments
description: This IRAD develops an Advanced Langmuir Probe (ALP) for flight on low perigee satellites carried out within high atomic oxygen environments such as Goddard’s proposed Atmosphere-Space Transition Region Explorer (ASTRE). This task consists of advancing Goddard’s existing Langmuir probe instrumentation to enable accurate swept I-V curves with minimum hysteresis in a highly atomic oxygen environment by optimizing the surface properties of the probe and by developing high voltage circuitry to ena

title: High-Resolution Plasma Analyzer for Ions
description: HYPERION is a top-hat electrostatic plasma analyzer to measure ionospheric plasmas and unprecedented mass and energy resolution. This IRAD will create an ion optics design for a high mass resolution plasma spectrometer.

title: Robust Split Electrical Control Design for Microshutter Arrays
description: The proposed work will upgrade the Next-Generation Microshutter Array 3-electrode design to a 4-electrode design to reduce voltage requirements from a 100-150 V range to an anticipated 60 V to open up currently limited electrical hardware options and elevate shutter actuation reliability to maintain long lifetime shutter success rate of shutters on long-duration science missions. A new fabrication process using bonded silicon-on-insulator (BSOI) wafers will be developed to fabricate verticall

title: Model-Based Safety and Mission Assurance Implementation (MBSMAI) and Testing
description: This is a cross-cutting process feasibility and improvement investment for digitally enabling SMA mission support capabilities and leverages Digital Engineering (DE) platforms (i.e., modeling tools and more) and capabilities from previous NASA investments. Model-Based Safety and Mission Assurance Implementation (MBSMAI) will answer the questions: Is Model-Based Engineering/Digital Engineering (MBE/DE) valid and usable for all SMA Engineering disciplines? What is needed to enable model-bas
technologyArea: Software, Modeling, Simulation, and Information Processing

title: Micro Magnetometers for Science
description: We propose to investigate the possibility of using commercially available magneto-resistive and/or magnetic-inductive sensors (e.g. the magnetometers typically used in smart phones) to conduct scientifically useful measurements in extremely resource-constrained space environments (i.e. mission designs where the available mass and power are << 1 kg and << 1 W).

title: LExSO Lidar Laser Development
description: We propose to demonstrate the new laser capabilities required for spectroscopic infrared reflectance measurement for high sensitivity mapping of water ice as well as for high-resolution topographic mapping.
technologyArea: Air, Water, Microbial, and Acoustic Sensors

title: A unified approach to retrieval of upper mesosphere and lower thermosphere winds for assimilation in GEOS-5
description: The Goddard Earth Observing System version (GEOS) is developed by NASA’s Global Modeling and Assimilation Office (GMAO). This project aims to produce a historical dataset of atmospheric neutral winds in the mesosphere and lower thermosphere (MLT; ~80-100 km altitude) in formats ready for integration into the vertically extended GEOS model. We will also collaborate on collating a homogenized and quality-controlled record of Earth observations.
technologyArea: Software, Modeling, Simulation, and Information Processing

title: Alignment and integration support for a new high-contrast integral field spectrograph
description:
An integral field spectrograph (IFS) camera may help fulfill the exoplanet characterization goals of a future Habitable Worlds Observatory. We will design a next-generation IFS prototype to be installed at on the DST2 coronagraph testbed at JPL's High Contrast Imaging Testbed facility. This new IFS will support TRL 4-5 demonstrations of IFS operation with coronagraphs at contrast levels needed to obtain the spectrum of an Earth-like exoplanet.

During 2023-2024 our team leveraged our eng

title: A-STEP: The AstroPix Sounding rocket Technology dEmonstration Payload
description: CMOS Monolithic Active Pixel Sensors (APSs) are low power, easy to fabricate, and can improve the sensitivity of gamma-ray telescopes in the keV to MeV energy regimes by an order of magnitude compared to current silicon technologies. GSFC’s investment in previous IRADs has yielded a successful ROSES proposal (APRA) that funded the development of such detectors for use in space, and a Roman Technology Fellowship (RTF) to develop an APS instrument as a hosted payload. The team has ident

title: Next Generation High-Bandwidth Telemetery and Accompanying Agile Multi-Instrument Smart Interface for Sounding Rocket Experiments
description: Our main objective is to develop a next generation centralized multi- instrument GSE that has the interface capability to use the 40 Mbps down link newly available at Wallops while remaining flexible enough to easily adapt to many instruments using documented procedures.

title: Sensorimotor Countermeasures
description: Exploration class missions will include multiple transitions between gravitational environments, sometimes after long periods in microgravity, which will impact the neurovestibular system and sensorimotor capabilities. New countermeasures and assessment tools are needed to enhance preflight disorientation training, maintain inflight physical performance, and accelerate recovery following transitions between gravity environments so that crew are enabled to perform critical exploration mission

title: Head/Body Monitor
description: Exploration class missions will require crew to be able to self-assess and treat their sensorimotor dysfunction after gravity transitions to accomplish operational tasks. To quantify crewmember’s adaptation following gravitational transitions and to inform when countermeasures may be needed, we must explore, develop, and validate unobtrusive monitoring tools (Monitoring Tools), define sensorimotor performance metrics (Metrics of Performance), and explore capabilities to enhance sensorimotor a
technologyArea: Human Health, Life Support, and Habitation Systems

title: Upright Proprioception Retention via Inflight Training and Evaluation
description: This project involves the development of an inflight sensorimotor countermeasure and assessment tool known as the Upright Proprioception Retention via Inflight Training and Evaluation (UPRITE) system. Specifically, an International Space Station (ISS) Technical Demonstration is planned as exploration candidate hardware that can eventually be used for operations. The use of an inflight countermeasure that prevents, or mitigates, the degradation of balance control mechanisms will improve postur

title: Spatial Disorientation Trainer
description: The purpose of this task is to develop a portable training system to be used preflight that will prepare crews for the vestibular disruptions typically experienced following g-transitions. This will be accomplished using the enhanced phase-2 funding to Soterix Medical Inc, who are developing an inertial measurement unit (IMU)-coupled galvanic vestibular stimulator (GVS) device under NASA Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR). Mars Campaign Office (MCO) is partnering with the Human Researc
technologyArea: Human Health, Life Support, and Habitation Systems

title: ICE CHILL: Integrated Cryo-operable Electronics to Condition Hibernation for Improved Li-ion Longevity
description:
ICE CHILL, a novel integrated battery module/battery management system
(BMS) and demonstrate its capability to enable passive Li-ion battery hibernation through the lunar 
night with zero excess degradation caused by freezing the cells. We will deliver a cryogenically 
operable battery management system, a 28V/15Ah battery module that can passively survive the 
lunar night, and a comprehensive Li-ion battery hibernation protocol for other future designs to 
follow. I

title: Eth-RT: Enabling Real-Time Ethernet Communication Between Commercial-Off-The-Shelf Devices
description: NASA relies on real-time Ethernet protocols for many programs.  These protocols rely on proprietary network cards and non-standard message formats that increase costs and limit interoperability.  This work will develop an Ethernet switch that enables real-time communication between standard Ethernet end nodes sending standard Ethernet messages

title: BrainStack: An Orbital Laboratory for AI/ML processors-including the first flight of NASA High Performance Spacecraft Computing (HPSC) Platform
description: This project builds upon previously flown experiments on TES-10, 13. They hosted the first LEO demonstration of a neuromorphic processor and NVIDIA GPU. These devices have been superceeded by powerful new variations but have yet to be flown in space. Previously developed TES-OS will be used to simplify the otherwise challenging interface between various candidate processors and TES-core avionics. This, combined with TES-n bus power/thermal hardware permits a modular platform for hosted AI/ML
